que: In Hilt, what is the primary goal?
options: replace testing entirely
provide dependencies with DI graph
avoid understanding requirements
skip state/data modeling

que: Which API/concept is most central to Hilt?
options: @HiltAndroidApp
random utility class name
legacy XML-only patterns unrelated to this topic
manual polling loop for all tasks

que: What is a recommended best practice for Hilt?
options: mix unrelated responsibilities in one function
ignore edge/error states
optimize before verifying correctness
constructor injection with @Inject

que: Which is a common mistake when implementing Hilt?
options: write focused tests for behavior
follow official docs for API usage
manually creating every dependency in activities
keep boundaries clear

que: For production code, which approach fits Hilt best?
options: Use Hilt while bypassing architecture boundaries
Use Hilt and suppress all warnings
Use Hilt only as theory with no implementation
Use Hilt with clear state flow and testable logic

que: A teammate asks where to start with Hilt. What is best advice?
options: Read official docs (https://developer.android.com/) and implement a small sample
skip running the app after changes
copy an unknown snippet without context
avoid feedback from tests/review

que: Which statement about Hilt is most accurate?
options: Hilt should be applied identically in every layer
Hilt is most effective when paired with clear ownership of state/data
Hilt removes the need for any testing
Hilt is mainly about adding boilerplate

que: In a code review for Hilt, what would you flag?
options: explicit modeling of UI/data states
traceable data flow between layers
Code that hides failures instead of handling them explicitly
small pure functions with clear names

que: Which scenario is the strongest example of using Hilt correctly?
options: A small, working feature that demonstrates @HiltAndroidApp in context
a slide deck with no runnable code
an implementation with no error states
a large refactor without tests

que: To learn Hilt deeply, what should you do next?
options: skip verification to save time
memorize definitions only
avoid official references
Build, test, profile (if needed), and iterate using Hilt patterns
